title: T-021 — UI: RPT-009 Integration Health (/reporting/integration-health) admin-only + DLQ detail (12-REPORTING §15.1 RPT-009, §12.2)
prompt:
# T-021 — RPT-009 Integration Health UI (admin-only)

PRD: docs/prd/12-REPORTING-PRD.md §15.1 RPT-009, §4.1 #9, §12.2, §12.3 dashboard alerts (DSL P2)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Prototype: prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/reporting/admin-screens.jsx:4-144 (`rpt_integration_health`)
UX: prototypes/design/12-REPORTING-UX.md:543-585

## Goal
Server Component page `/reporting/integration-health` (admin-only via middleware T-009 — role check + `rpt.integration.read` permission). Renders: 6 stage cards (P1: 1/2/3/5; P2: 4/6 disabled with badge), DLQ detail table (only when hasDlq), latency multi-line chart (24h rolling), KPI summary row, 'Go to DLQ Admin' cross-module link (`/admin/integrations/d365/dlq` — verify route exists or stub disabled).

## Prototype parity
- Source: `admin-screens.jsx:4-144`
- Translation: role !== 'Admin' early-return → middleware-level guard (T-009); RPT_IH_STAGES + RPT_IH_KPIS → T-014 integration-health service helper; ChartMultiLine → Recharts LineChart multi-series.

## Implementation contract
1. Page is admin-route via `apps/web/app/(admin)/reporting/integration-health/page.tsx` (or under `/(reporting)/admin/integration-health` if route group differs — verify existing admin route convention before implementation).
2. Middleware enforces `role IN ('admin','reporting_admin') AND permission rpt.integration.read`.
3. RSC reads T-014 getIntegrationHealth; client renders stages + DLQ table + multi-line latency chart.
4. DLQ rows conditional (hasDlq); 'Go to DLQ Admin' button gated on route existence.
5. Empty / loading / error / permission states; RTL + Playwright parity.
6. next-intl `reporting.integration_health.*` keys.

## Files
- apps/web/app/(reporting)/integration-health/page.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(reporting)/integration-health/_components/integration-health-client.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(reporting)/_components/charts/multi-line-chart.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(reporting)/integration-health/__tests__/integration-health.test.tsx [create]
- apps/web/messages/{en,pl}/reporting-integration-health.json [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a non-admin user requests /reporting/integration-health, when middleware T-009 runs, then 403 + audit row written; non-admin sees /403 page (NOT the integration-health UI).
2. Given admin user with rpt.integration.read permission accesses the page, when it renders, then 6 stage cards visible (4 P1 healthy/critical states + 2 P2 disabled), latency multi-line chart, KPI summary row.
3. Given an outbox stage has dlq_depth > 0, when page renders, then DLQ Detail table appears and 'Go to DLQ Admin' button is enabled; when all dlq_depth=0, DLQ section is absent.
4. Given latency_log has 24h rolling data, when ChartMultiLine renders, then it shows one line series per stage with x-axis hour-bucketed.
5. Given mv_integration_health is empty (no rows), when page renders, then empty state 'No integration data available' message shown.
6. UI parity + 4 states + next-intl wrapping.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: RTL covers admin-only guard + DLQ conditional + chart render + empty state.
- Playwright parity.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web test integration-health && pnpm --filter @monopilot/web playwright test reporting-integration-health`.
- GREEN: build page + middleware integration + chart + client.

## Risk red lines
- Do not rely on client-side `role !== 'Admin'` early return — middleware path-level guard only (per translation-notes RBAC red line).
- Do not allow DLQ replay from this UI (P2 — admin/integrations module owns mutations).
- Do not paste prototype JSX verbatim.
- Do not skip next-intl wrapping.
- Do not skip 4 states.
- Do not let 'Go to DLQ Admin' navigate to non-existent route — gate on route existence.
